analysis: The stronger heuristics employ multi‐stage approaches that combine fast greedy selection with localized exact-refinement, leveraging lazy evaluation, pre-computed Monte Carlo samples, and Pareto/pruning techniques to minimize redundant computations. They use vectorized operations and heap-based lazy updates for marginal gains, striking a balance between quality and speed, whereas the weaker ones often rely on more exhaustive or less efficient swap and recomputation strategies.
experience: Focus on reusing intermediate data (e.g., pre-computed masks and samples), implement lazy updates to avoid full re-evaluations, and incorporate quick filtering (like Pareto/pruning) to reduce candidate space for subsequent precise refinements.
current_reflection: Focus on reusing intermediate data (e.g., pre-computed masks and samples), implement lazy updates to avoid full re-evaluations, and incorporate quick filtering (like Pareto/pruning) to reduce candidate space for subsequent precise refinements.
bad_reflections: Focus on candidate pruning, reuse of sampled data, and vectorized incremental updates. Minimize repeated expensive hypervolume calls by using approximate evaluations and limited local refinements on shortlisted candidates.

Focus on data reuse and vectorized updates. Use shared samples and lazy evaluations for marginal gains while combining aggressive candidate pruning with local refinements to improve both accuracy and runtime.

Focus on aggressive pruning and vectorized, lazy-updating of contribution estimates. Reuse sampled data for both approximation and local search. Integrate global greedy selection with a focused exact-swap phase on a shortlist to cut overhead while still polishing hypervolume performance.
comprehensive_reflection:
• Keywords: Reuse, lazy updates, quick filtering, Pareto pruning.  
• Advice: Reuse intermediate data (e.g., pre-computed masks and samples) and perform lazy updates to avoid complete re-evaluations.  
• Avoid: Extensive full-scale re-evaluations and heavy reliance on global candidate evaluation without initial filtering.  
• Explanation: Combining incremental updates with early candidate pruning reduces computational load by narrowing the search space for precise refinements, thereby optimizing runtime and accuracy.
